## What this PR does

One or two sentences describing the change.

## Why

What problem does this solve, or what gap does it close? Link to an issue if there is one.

## Type of change

- [ ] Documentation fix (typo, broken link, factual correction)
- [ ] Documentation improvement (clarification, better example, restructuring)
- [ ] New template or new methodology section
- [ ] Change to existing methodology (this needs strong justification)
- [ ] Tooling, repo hygiene, or CI change
- [ ] Other (describe)

## Files changed

List the files this PR touches and a one-line note on each.

## Scope check

- [ ] This change is tool-agnostic (no assumptions about Claude, Cursor, Copilot, etc.)
- [ ] This change is stack-agnostic (no assumptions about .NET, React, Python, etc. — stack-specific content belongs in fse-extensions)
- [ ] This change is consistent with the existing tone and style of the repository
- [ ] This change does not introduce marketing language, hype, or aspirational claims

## Tested against real work

FSE changes should be grounded in real project experience, not theory. Briefly describe the project or situation that motivated this change. You don't have to share anything proprietary.

## Breaking change?

Does this change the meaning, intent, or required behavior of any existing FSE rule, template, or document? If yes, explain what downstream adopters would need to do.

## Checklist

- [ ] I have read CONTRIBUTING.md
- [ ] I have read CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
- [ ] I am submitting under Apache 2.0 (the project's license)
- [ ] I am the author of this content, or I have the right to contribute it

## Anything else

Notes for the reviewer, open questions, or things you're unsure about.
